---
title: "About"
title-block-style: none
pagetitle: "About Dr Mesfin Genie"
description: "Biography, expertise and professional background of Dr Mesfin Genie."
page-layout: full
image: /assets/img/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<header class="page-header"><div class="page-shell narrow"><p class="eyebrow">About</p><h1>Health economics, behavioural economics and decision evidence</h1><p class="lead">Dr Mesfin Genie is a Continuing Senior Lecturer in Health Economics at Newcastle Business School, The University of Newcastle, Australia.</p></div></header>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell split-grid"><div class="prose"><h2>Biography</h2><p>Dr Mesfin Genie studies how patients and the public value healthcare, process information and respond to policy. His work combines health economics, behavioural economics, stated-preference methods, discrete choice experiments, eye tracking, health measurement and decision-support tools.</p><p>He received his PhD in Economics from Ca’ Foscari University of Venice, where he used stated-preference methods to study healthcare decision-making. He has held research and teaching positions at Ca’ Foscari University of Venice, the Health Economics Research Unit at the University of Aberdeen, the Harrison School of Pharmacy at Auburn University, and the School of Medicine at Duke University.</p><h2>Research focus</h2><div class="inline-tags"><span class="tag">Health economics</span><span class="tag">Behavioural economics</span><span class="tag">Discrete choice experiments</span><span class="tag">Eye tracking</span><span class="tag">Health-preference research</span><span class="tag">Clinical outcomes assessment</span><span class="tag">Public-health policy</span><span class="tag">Decision-support tools</span></div><h2>Selected academic service</h2><p>Guest Editor for <em>Health Policy and Technology</em>, and reviewer for journals including <em>Journal of Health Economics</em>, <em>Health Economics</em>, <em>Value in Health</em>, <em>Social Science & Medicine</em>, <em>Journal of Choice Modelling</em>, <em>Theory and Decision</em>, and <em>BMJ Open</em>.</p></div><aside><h2>Education</h2><div class="timeline"><div class="timeline-item"><strong>2019</strong><br>PhD in Economics<br><span class="muted">Ca’ Foscari University of Venice, Italy</span></div><div class="timeline-item"><strong>2015</strong><br>MSc in Health Economics and Management<br><span class="muted">University of Bologna, Italy</span></div><div class="timeline-item"><strong>2010</strong><br>MSc in Economics<br><span class="muted">Addis Ababa University, Ethiopia</span></div><div class="timeline-item"><strong>2006</strong><br>BA in Economics<br><span class="muted">Jimma University, Ethiopia</span></div></div><h2>Prospective students</h2><p>I welcome enquiries in health economics, discrete choice experiments, eye-tracking methods, clinical outcomes assessment and behavioural economics. Please include a CV, grading information, research experience and publications where applicable.</p><p><a href="/supervision/">Supervision portfolio →</a></p></aside></div></section>
```
